definitions: Application Programming Interface
A way for two programs to talk to each other (exchange data/information)
The interface part of the backend that frontend talks to
Written inside backend code — it is a backend component
note: The 'two programs' can be any two separate pieces of software that need to communicate (e.g. your extension + your backend, your backend + OpenAI, weather app + weather service). They are separate because they have different codebases and run independently.

types.name: Backend API
types.description: You build your own in your server for connecting with client/frontend, like in Node.js

types.name: External API
types.description: Someone else's backend that you call, like OpenAI, a weather service, or a maps service

types.name: Browser API (Frontend APIs)
types.description: Built into the browser to connect with servers, like fetch() and localStorage()

definitions: Asking another service for data
Temporarily stored data to speed up future requests
The instructions you write that create programs
Text you write in a programming language to give instructions to the computer
Code is static (just text sitting in files) — when you run/execute it, it becomes a program
The complete collection of all source code files for a project or program
All the code files that make up your project, stored in one place
note: Codebase = the actual code files themselves (the content). File structure = how those files are organized (the organization).

definitions: Putting your project online for others to use
Secret config values like API keys and passwords stored outside your code
How the code files are organized into folders and subfolders
A complete skeleton/structure for building applications that enforces specific patterns and controls the overall flow of your program
The framework calls YOUR code, not the other way around (inversion of control)
Opinionated — has strong rules about how you should organize and write code
Provides the entire foundation, not just individual tools

types.name: Node.js
types.description: Express.js (web server), Next.js (full-stack React apps), NestJS (enterprise applications)

types.name: Python
types.description: Django (full-stack web framework), Flask (lightweight web framework), FastAPI (modern API framework)

types.name: Frontend
types.description: React (UI framework), Vue (UI framework), Angular (UI framework)

definitions: Frontend + Backend combined
HyperText Transfer Protocol — the rules/language browsers and servers use to talk to each other
The protocol used between client and server

types.name: GET
types.description: Retrieve/read data — asking the server to send you information

types.name: POST
types.description: Create/send new data — sending data to server to create something new

types.name: PUT
types.description: Update/replace entire resource — replacing all data of something with new data

types.name: PATCH
types.description: Partially update resource — updating just part of the data

types.name: DELETE
types.description: Remove resource — delete data from server

types.name: HEAD
types.description: Same as GET but only retrieves headers (no body) — used to check if a resource exists without downloading it

types.name: OPTIONS
types.description: Ask server what methods are allowed — checks CORS (Cross Origin Resource Sharing)

types.name: CONNECT
types.description: Establish a tunnel (for proxies) — used for very specific networking scenarios

types.name: TRACE
types.description: Echo back the request (for debugging) — rarely used, mostly for troubleshooting

note: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE are the five most important ones.

definitions: JavaScript Object Notation — a format for organizing and sending data (looks like JavaScript objects)
A universal language that both frontend and backend understand
A collection of pre-written modules that solve common problems — someone else already wrote the code, tested it, and packaged it for you to use
You are in control — you decide when and how to use it
Passive — it sits there waiting for you to call it
Modular — you can use just the parts you need

types.name: Node.js
types.description: axios (HTTP requests), mysql2 (database connections), moment (date manipulation), bcrypt (password hashing)

types.name: Python
types.description: requests (HTTP requests), pandas (data analysis), numpy (numerical computing), matplotlib (plotting/graphs), beautifulsoup (web scraping)

definitions: Your own computer acting as a server
A single, focused piece of code that does ONE specific thing — the smallest building block
Single responsibility: does one job only
Self-contained: all the code for that one job is in one place
Reusable: you can use it anywhere in your project
Importable: you bring it into other files when needed

types.name: Node.js built-in
types.description: fs (file operations), path (file paths), os (operating system info), crypto (encryption)

types.name: Python built-in
types.description: math (mathematical functions), random (random numbers), datetime (date/time handling), json (JSON parsing)

types.name: Your own
types.description: helpers.js / utils.py — your utility functions

definitions: A bundle of code (modules/libraries/frameworks) that's been packaged up and published for others to download and use
Code you write → package it up → publish to npm → others can install it
note: npm (Node Package Manager) is like an app store for JavaScript code. Package managers by language: JavaScript/Node.js uses npm, yarn, or pnpm. Python uses pip or conda.

definitions: A door number the server listens on (like 3000, 5000)
A set of instructions that tell the computer what to do
Running/executing a program: the process of the computer following the instructions step-by-step

types.name: Applications (Apps)
types.description: Programs you use directly — browser, Instagram, browser extension

types.name: System Software
types.description: Programs that run the computer itself — Windows, macOS, Linux

types.name: Scripts/Code
types.description: Small programs that do specific tasks — a JavaScript file that checks URLs, a Python script that analyzes data

definitions: What the client (frontend) sends to the server (backend) asking for something
What the server sends back to the client after processing the request

parts.name: Status Code
parts.description: Did it work? (200 = success, 404 = not found, 500 = error)

example:
Status: 200 OK
Body: { "safe": true, "reason": "No threats detected" }

definitions: The complete ecosystem needed to run your code — includes interpreter/compiler + libraries + APIs + memory management + everything else

parts.name: Memory Management
parts.description: Handles memory allocation and garbage collection

types.name: Node.js
types.description: V8 engine + Node APIs + event loop

types.name: Python Runtime
types.description: Python interpreter + standard library

types.name: JVM
types.description: Java Virtual Machine — includes Java compiler + runtime libraries

definitions: Programs and instructions that tell a computer what to do — the non-physical part of a computer
note: Software is a general term for all programs. A program is a specific piece of software. An application (app) is software you use directly. Software is a group of multiple programs, though a single program can be called software too.

definitions: The combination of technologies and tools you use to build a project
Software used during development to write, run, test, and manage code

types.name: Node.js
types.description: JavaScript runtime — lets you run JavaScript outside the browser

types.name: npm
types.description: Package manager — installs libraries/tools (comes with Node.js)

types.name: VS Code
types.description: Code editor

types.name: Git
types.description: Version control — saves your code history, enables teamwork

types.name: GitHub
types.description: Code hosting — store code online, share with team

types.name: Postman
types.description: API tester — test your backend without building frontend yet

types.name: MySQL Workbench
types.description: Database viewer — see your database visually

types.name: Chrome DevTools
types.description: Browser debugger — see errors, test code in browser

types.name: Terminal/Command Prompt
types.description: Command line — run commands to start servers, install things

definitions: Uniform Resource Locator — the address/location of something on the internet
note: URL path: the part of the URL that comes AFTER the domain name, specifying which specific resource/page you want. Example: in 'example.com/check-url', the path is '/check-url'.
